name: Melilla (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the field of the city's arms, derived from those of the House of Medina Sidonia and the Marquess of the Vélez
details of the field and the chequered border
details of the chequered border and ornaments of the coat of arms
details of the coat of arms (lions, castles and ornaments)

description: A blue field bearing the city's heraldic coat of arms in the centre, framed by a checkered red and white border. The arms recall the lordship of the House of Medina Sidonia, who took the city in 1497.
history: Melilla, a Spanish autonomous city on the Mediterranean coast of North Africa, has been Spanish since 1497. Its flag is derived from the heraldry of the Dukes of Medina Sidonia (the Guzman family), who led the city's conquest. The flag was officially adopted with the city's Statute of Autonomy in 1995.

features.symbolism: Quartered field with the arms of the Marquess of the Vélez (azure and azure with a chequered border), bearing the coat of arms of Melilla in the centre.

features.role: coat of arms of Melilla, derived from the arms of the Dukes of Medina Sidonia
features.symbolism: the historical lordship of the Dukes of Medina Sidonia (House of Guzman), who conquered Melilla for Spain in 1497, with their characteristic chequered (gironny) border.
